summary: Round-3 research complete. HEADLINE (Q1): the existing UPower 2% backstop (CriticalPowerAction=HybridSleep, PercentageAction=2) is DENIED by systemd-logind while Claude Code's strong block inhibitors are held - verified end-to-end from installed-version sources (UPower 1.91.1 legacy HybridSleep(false) call cannot carry SD_LOGIND_SKIP_INHIBITORS; systemd v260 verify_shutdown_creds rejects outright on any strong block inhibitor, root gets no exemption; UPower fires once with a NULL error callback, never retries, never falls back) and corroborated by 7 live BlockedByInhibitorLock journal denials this boot. Consequence: with agents active, battery would drain past 2% to hard power loss. (B) verdict: 'sleep after an hour without agent activity' is UNACHIEVABLE as stated - the inhibitors come from the user's own SessionStart/SessionEnd hooks (repo: config/claude/settings.json) and are held while a session is merely open at the prompt; additionally gsd's battery idle-suspend is one-shot per idle period (no retry after a denial). Closest approximation: turn-scoped hooks (UserPromptSubmit/Stop) + sleep-inactive-battery-timeout 900->3600 + explicit sleep-inactive-battery-type=suspend, with residual misses bounded by the ~20% battery-low re-arm and the 10% backstop. (C) design: new root systemd timer (60s) polling /sys/class/power_supply/BAT*/ and running 'systemctl suspend -i' at <=10% while Discharging - verified bypass chain (systemctl -i => SuspendWithFlags(SD_LOGIND_SKIP_INHIBITORS); polkit implicitly authorizes uid 0). Threshold 10% not 2% (s2idle-only platform, 61Wh battery, FW13 AMD community drain 1.5-3%/h => 2% dies in under an hour suspended). Hibernate/HybridSleep NOT dependable (16G swapfile vs 30G RAM with ~28G routinely used, despite systemd-initrd shipping hibernate-resume) - use suspend. (A) confirmed no regression (power.nix lock committed; live logind still pre-activation 'suspend'). Also found: orphaned-inhibitor hazard (detached 'sleep infinity' survives missed SessionEnd until reboot).

artifacts.summary: Round-3: UPower 2% backstop verified blocked (headline); per-requirement A/B/C verdicts; concrete 10% root-timer suspend -i backstop spec for power.nix; turn-scoped hook recommendation; hibernate non-viability; verified-fact vs inference ledger with upstream URLs and live evidence

continuation_context: Ready for /plan (or /revise of plan 01) integrating report 03. Implementation surface: (1) modules/system/power.nix - add battery-suspend-backstop systemd service+timer (spec in report Section 6, threshold 10, BAT* glob only - peripheral hid-* battery false-positive documented); (2) modules/home/desktop/gnome.nix - sleep-inactive-battery-timeout 900->3600 and explicit sleep-inactive-battery-type="suspend"; (3) config/claude/settings.json - move inhibitor acquisition SessionStart->UserPromptSubmit, release on Stop (+SessionEnd belt-and-suspenders), and tie inhibitor lifetime to the claude process instead of detached 'sleep infinity' (orphan hazard); (4) docs touch-ups per prior rounds' targets. User decisions to surface at plan time: 10% vs other threshold; whether to adopt turn-scoped hooks (weakens protection for run_in_background jobs); optional retrying idle-suspend poller extension (report Section 3.3, not recommended initially). Phase-4 activation of the lid lock config still pending user sudo (unchanged).
next_steps: Run /plan 117 (revision) to fold report 03 into an implementation plan; lid-lock activation via sudo nixos-rebuild switch remains a user step
